case_name: ROCBA — Fred Rocba IP Theft (SANS FOR500 Showcase, May 2026)
source: SANS Rob T. Lee, released 2026-05-26 via Egnyte
key_questions: What key projects did Fred Rocba have access to?

expected.significance: Primary user account — all artifact paths anchor here

iocs.rationale: Fred Rocba is the sole user; profile existence confirms correct image

expected.significance: Office 365 Exchange account linked to SRL — confirms SRL access

iocs.rationale: Email configured per slide 5; OST or registry mail profile confirms SRL O365 access

expected.significance: SRL IP files on local disk — establishes what was accessible

iocs.rationale: Case question 1: what projects did Rocba have access to — answered by local SRL docs

expected.significance: External storage connected during break-in window (2020-11-13)

iocs.rationale: Case question 4: how was it stolen — USB exfiltration is primary hypothesis for physical break-in

expected.significance: Drive letter assigned to USB device — corroborates USBSTOR entry

iocs.rationale: Case question 3: where transferred — drive letter assignment narrows exfil destination

expected.significance: LNK files created during break-in confirm which files were opened

iocs.rationale: Case questions 2+5: what was stolen and when — LNK timestamps pin activity to 2020-11-13
Case question 4: how stolen — prefetch shows what tools intruder ran on the system

expected.significance: Folders browsed during break-in — identifies which SRL directories were accessed

iocs.rationale: Case question 1+2: shellbags show folder navigation to SRL project directories

expected.significance: Recently accessed files — direct list of what intruder opened

iocs.rationale: Case question 2: what was stolen — MRU is the most direct answer for file access

expected.significance: OneDrive personal and business configured per slide 5 — potential cloud exfil path

iocs.rationale: Case question 3: where transferred — cloud sync as alternative to USB exfil

expected.significance: Slide 6 confirms vacation photos synced to home system — corroborates Fred was absent

iocs.rationale: Establishes Fred was genuinely away — rules out Fred as suspect for 2020-11-13 activity

expected.significance: MFT / USN journal entries anchoring all activity to break-in date

iocs.rationale: Case question 5: when — 2020-11-13 is the known break-in date per slide 3
